title: Late-Life Gene Therapy Extends Mouse Lifespan 20% — Even When Started Old
description: A landmark study covered by Lifespan.io and Longevity Today shows a single late-life gene therapy boosted median lifespan from 28 to 34 months in mice (≈20.5%) while improving glucose tolerance, fitness, and cognition. Because the intervention started late in life, it powerfully argues that aging is reversible mid-stream, not just preventable — a key proof-of-concept for rejuvenation therapeutics in older humans.

title: 25HC Identified as a Naturally Occurring Senolytic That Restores 23% of Aged Muscle Mass
description: Buck Institute's Melov lab, publishing in iScience, showed five days of 25-hydroxycholesterol (25HC) injection in aged mice cleared senescent cells and restored 23% of muscle mass — with activity against senescent human heart, liver, kidney, and cartilage cells. Because 25HC is endogenous, it positions a body-native molecule as the next-generation senolytic candidate alongside ABT-263 and fisetin.

title: Rapalogix Health Raises $20M Series A to Build Longevity-First Topical Rapamycin Platform
description: Rapalogix Health closed a $20M Series A to commercialize Re-Q, its topical mTOR-modulator for skin biological aging, with a broader longevity pipeline behind it. The round is the largest aimed at translating rapamycin-class biology into a daily-use consumer-medical product and signals deepening investor appetite for clinical-grade longevity dermatology.

title: Cambrian Biopharma Reports Positive Human Phase 1b Data for ATX-304 Aging-Pathway Drug
description: Per Longevity.Technology, Cambrian Bio disclosed Phase 1b human data validating ATX-304's ability to restore a key metabolic pathway tied to aging — one of the first geroscience-platform candidates to deliver positive human readout. The result reinforces that geroprotector drug development is moving from animal models into bona fide clinical territory.

title: WEF Calls Low-Tech Longevity the $6 Trillion Opportunity to 2040
description: At the New Champions meeting in Dalian, the World Economic Forum estimated low-tech, low-cost longevity strategies could prevent 400M home falls, 8.5M type-2 diabetes cases, and 2.4M dementia cases by 2040 — unlocking $5.8T in healthcare savings and $645B in productivity. The framing legitimizes lifestyle/longevity infrastructure as an economic priority, not just a wellness category.

title: Late-Life Gene Therapy Boosts Mouse Lifespan by 20%
description: Single late-life intervention raised mouse median lifespan from 28 to 34 months while improving glucose tolerance, fitness, and cognition — strong evidence rejuvenation works post-onset.

title: 25HC Acts as Naturally Occurring Senolytic, Restores 23% of Aged Muscle Mass
description: Buck Institute's Melov lab shows 25-hydroxycholesterol clears senescent cells and rejuvenates aged muscle in mice, with activity in human heart, liver, kidney, and cartilage cells.

title: Rapalogix Health Raises $20M Series A for Longevity-Based Skin Health
description: Series A funds commercialization of Re-Q topical mTOR-modulator and broader longevity dermatology pipeline.

title: Cambrian Bio's ATX-304: Positive Human Phase 1b Data Validates Aging Target
description: Phase 1b results show ATX-304 restores a metabolic pathway tied to aging in humans, a notable early clinical win for a geroscience-platform drug.

title: WEF: Low-Tech Longevity Could Unlock $6 Trillion by 2040
description: WEF estimate at the Dalian meeting projects 400M fall, 8.5M diabetes, and 2.4M dementia cases prevented through low-cost longevity strategies.

title: Fight Aging Newsletter — June 22, 2026
description: Weekly digest covers macrophage MMP9 clearance for bone aging, BMAL1 macrophage lever, combination senolytic + stem cell mouse data, GRK2 Alzheimer's, and StackAge multi-omics aging clock.

title: UCL Launches First Human Trial of T-Cell Rejuvenation Therapy
description: UCL-led first-in-human trial of an immune rejuvenation therapy designed to restore worn-out T cell function is expected to begin later this year.

title: Voyager Therapeutics Gets FDA Clearance for First-Ever Tau Gene Therapy
description: FDA clears Voyager's tau-targeting gene therapy for Alzheimer's — a strategic pivot from amyloid-focused approaches that signals broader regulatory acceptance of tau biology.

title: Gero Raises $17M, Expands Chugai Collaboration for Physics-Based Aging Drug Discovery
description: Gero's $17M round and expanded Chugai deal validates physics-based, human-data-driven aging target identification as an investable platform.

title: Eli Lilly Signs $1B+ Deal with AlzeCure for Preventive Alzheimer's Therapy
description: Lilly's $1B+ AlzeCure partnership targets prodromal Alzheimer's — preventive neurology becomes mainstream big-pharma investment.

title: Fractyl Health REVEAL-1: 78% GLP-1 Weight Loss Maintained After Revita Procedure
description: One-year REVEAL-1 data shows patients keep ~78% of GLP-1-linked weight loss after Revita — a potential clinical off-ramp from Ozempic-class drugs.

title: Rejuvenate Biomed Completes Enrollment in Phase 2 COPD-Sarcopenia Trial
description: RJx-01 Phase 2 readout pending — a clinical sarcopenia drug aimed at frailty and muscle loss in older adults.

title: Istesso Launches Phase 2 Trial of Leramistat for Sarcopenia
description: Phase 2 sarcopenia trial advances the second large clinical program directly targeting age-related muscle loss this month.

title: Semaglutide Slows Epigenetic Aging by 9% on DunedinPACE in Phase 2b
description: 32-week randomized Phase 2b trial in HIV-linked lipohypertrophy patients shows ~9% slowing of biological aging pace versus placebo on DunedinPACE clock.

title: Long-Lived Families Reveal Rare Genetic Variants That Tame Inflammation
description: Genetic variants in long-lived families tied to lower chronic inflammation — a clue to drug targets for healthier human aging.

title: Yale: Nearly Half of Adults Over 65 Improve Physically or Mentally With Age
description: 12-year Yale study of 11,000+ adults finds 45% improve cognitively or physically after 65, challenging linear-decline aging narratives.

title: Heliconius Butterfly Lifespan Genetics Could Unlock Anti-Aging Pathways
description: Heliconius butterflies live several times longer than relatives with little physical decline — a new comparative model species for human longevity research.

title: Phosphatidylcholine Decline Identified as a Reversible Driver of Aging Cells
description: Boosting phosphatidylcholine restores youthful mitochondrial function in aged tissues, suggesting a new actionable nutritional aging target.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Reports Topical GPX4-Modulator Senolytic Clinical Data
description: RLS1496 induces selective ferroptosis in senescent cells while sparing stressed cells — a precision senolytic moves further into clinical development.

title: China Launches 2,000-Patient Stem Cell Aging Trial at 301 Hospital, Beijing
description: One of the largest stem cell aging trials ever launched — assessing function preservation during aging at the People's Liberation Army General Hospital.

title: Linnaeus' LNS8801 Enters NIA Interventions Testing Program
description: Linnaeus Therapeutics' GPER agonist LNS8801 will be evaluated in the National Institute on Aging's gold-standard mouse lifespan testing program.

title: NIH Identifies Self-Reinforcing Loop Driving Tissue Aging
description: NIH scientists reveal a positive-feedback loop between cellular damage, inflammation, and senescence that drives accelerating tissue aging.

title: Yale 12-Year Study: 45% of Older Adults Get Sharper or Stronger Over Time
description: Adults with positive aging beliefs gained physical or cognitive function after 65, suggesting psychology meaningfully shapes biological trajectories.

title: Peter Attia + Brian Kennedy Podcast: What May Actually Slow Aging
description: Drive Podcast roundtable with Brian Kennedy maps interventions with the strongest current evidence base — including rapamycin, exercise, and sleep architecture.

title: Peter Attia Hosts Longevity Roundtable on Strength, Protein, and Training
description: Roundtable framework crystalizes resistance training and protein dosing as the strongest current healthspan levers.

title: Strength Training Sweet Spot: 90-120 Minutes Weekly Cuts All-Cause Mortality 13%
description: 147,000-person British Journal of Sports Medicine analysis: 90-119 min/week of resistance training tied to 13% lower all-cause, 19% lower CV, and 27% lower neurological mortality.

title: 20-Year Lifestyle vs Drug Trial: Lifestyle Cuts Multimorbidity Risk 21%
description: Two-decade follow-up of the Diabetes Prevention Program shows lifestyle arm developed 21% fewer cases of multimorbidity (≥2 chronic diseases) than drug controls.

title: Oura Rolls Out Counsel Health AI + Clinician Care Inside the App
description: Oura members in 43 US states get free in-app medical AI chat plus optional licensed-physician visits — a major step in wearable-to-clinical integration.

title: Canyon Ranch Tucson Launches $20K Dedicated Longevity Retreat
description: Flagship destination spa formalizes longevity into a structured multi-day program with 30+ daily activities priced at ~$20K/person — legacy hospitality embraces clinical-grade longevity.

title: Samsung Health Adds AI Energy Score and AGEs (Glycation) Index
description: Redesigned Samsung Health app rolls out an AGEs Index that runs automatically during sleep — bringing biological-age-style metrics to mainstream phones.
